items.title: Linux may be ending support for older network drivers due to influx of false AI-generated bug reports — maintenance has become too burdensome for old largely-unused systems
items.description: Linux kernel developers are reviewing a proposal to remove obsolete ISA and PCMCIA-era Ethernet drivers from the mainline kernel, citing rising maintenance burden from AI-driven bug reports and fuzzing. The change would cut around 27,000 lines of legacy code

Subscribe to our newsletter The Linux kernel community is currently debating a significant proposal that could see countless legacy network drivers purged from the mainline source code to combat an unsustainable surge in AI-driven bug reports. This development follows a patch series submitted by OG developer Andrew Lunn to the netdev mailing list earlier this week.Maintaining support for old hardware has always been a “thing” for Linux. However, thanks to AI-wielding “detectives,” the sheer number of reports is forcing a shift in the kernel’s long-standing philosophy. Developers must now choose between addressing countless low-quality or hallucinated reports on systems no one uses or focusing their limited time on modern, high-impact subsystems.Andrew Lunn argued that while support for aging ISA and PCMCIA-era hardware was once a low-maintenance endeavor, it has recently become a disproportionate burden due to newbies using AI and fuzzers to uncover theoretical defects in code that likely have no remaining active users.Article continues below You may like Linux devs start removing support for 37-year-old Intel 486 CPU Linux lays down the law on AI-generated code, says yes to Copilot, no to AI slop, and humans take the fall for mistakes Linux kernel 7.0 finally abandons the 28-year-old Intel 440BX chipset "These old drivers have not been much of a maintenance burden until recently,” writes Lunn. “Now there are more newbies are using AI and fuzzers to find issues, resulting in more work for Maintainers. Fixing these old drivers makes little sense if it is not clear they have users.” Linux patches to remove ancient network drivers from kernel source tree (Image credit: Linux Kernel Community)Lunn notes that many of the Ethernet devices date to the late 1900s and feature ISA or PCMCIA interfaces (although there are a few that debuted between 2001 and 2002). If accepted, Lunn's proposal would remove specific drivers from 3Com, AMD, SMSC, Cirrus Logic, Fujitsu, Xircom, and 8390-based hardware families, eliminating approximately 27,646 lines of code from the kernel source tree.More importantly, rather than nuking support all at once, Linux would handle the removal one patch at a time, meaning a user could restore any of these drivers if they still depend on them and are willing to step in as a maintainer. This approach would ensure that legacy systems are not permanently locked out, but no longer impose an ongoing maintenance burden by default. items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: Linux或因AI生成错误报告激增而停止支持旧网络驱动——老旧系统维护已不堪重负
items.descriptionZh:
Linux内核社区近期正围绕一项重大提案展开激烈讨论，该提案旨在从主线源代码中清除大量遗留的网络驱动程序，以应对由人工智能（AI）驱动的错误报告数量激增所带来的不可持续的压力。这一动向源于资深开发者Andrew Lunn本周早些时候向netdev邮件列表提交的一系列补丁。

长期以来，为老旧硬件提供支持一直是Linux内核的核心理念之一。然而，随着越来越多新手开发者利用AI工具和模糊测试（Fuzzers）进行代码检测，情况发生了根本性变化。这些“AI侦探”能够自动发现大量理论上的代码缺陷，导致针对那些可能已无活跃用户的古老硬件的错误报告数量呈爆炸式增长。这使得内核维护者面临艰难抉择：是将有限的开发时间耗费在修复这些低质量甚至可能是AI“幻觉”产生的、无关紧要的报告上，还是集中精力维护现代、高性能且影响广泛的核心子系统。

Andrew Lunn在提案中指出，尽管支持那些基于ISA和PCMCIA等上古接口的硬件曾是一项维护成本很低的工作，但近期它已变成了不成比例的负担。他写道：“这些旧驱动程序直到最近都不是什么维护负担。但现在有更多新手使用AI和模糊测试来发现问题，这给维护者带来了更多工作。如果无法明确这些驱动是否还有用户，修复它们就意义不大。”

具体而言，此次清理目标主要针对上世纪90年代末期及21世纪初（少数到2002年）的以太网设备驱动，涉及3Com、AMD、SMSC、Cirrus Logic、富士通、Xircom以及基于8390芯片的硬件系列。如果提案获得通过，预计将从内核源码树中删除约27,646行代码。

值得注意的是，Linux社区计划采取的并非“一刀切”的激进策略。移除工作将通过逐个提交补丁的方式稳步推进。这意味着，如果仍有用户依赖某个特定驱动，并且愿意站出来承担维护责任，他们可以恢复该驱动。这种渐进式方法既确保了那些仍在运行的遗留系统不会被永久拒之门外，又能在默认情况下将内核维护者从这些持续的低价值维护任务中解放出来，从而优化整体开发资源的分配。

这一事件标志着Linux开发文化在AI时代面临的新挑战：当自动化工具极大降低了发现潜在问题的门槛时，项目必须重新评估其“支持一切”的包容性哲学与维护效率之间的平衡。其影响深远，不仅关乎内核代码库的“瘦身”与现代化，也可能为其他大型开源项目如何应对AI生成的海量问题报告树立先例。最终，决策将引导开发资源流向对现代计算生态更具价值的创新与优化工作。

items.title: Now Meta will track what employees do on their computers to train its AI agents
items.description:
Meta employees' activity at work is now being used to train the company's AI agents. As reported by Reuters, Meta is installing a tool it calls Model Capability Initiative (MCI) on US-based employees' computers that runs in work-related apps and websites, recording mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and occasional screenshots. 
The data from this tool will be used to train the company's AI models to get better at interacting with computers the way humans do, including automating work tasks like those Meta's employees perform on the job. According to Reuters, the data from MCI won't be "used for performance assessments."

Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Meta employees’ activity at work is now being used to train the company’s AI agents. As reported by Reuters, Meta is installing a tool it calls Model Capability Initiative (MCI) on US-based employees’ computers that runs in work-related apps and websites, recording mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and occasional screenshots.The data from this tool will be used to train the company’s AI models to get better at interacting with computers the way humans do, including automating work tasks like those Meta’s employees perform on the job. According to Reuters, the data from MCI won’t be “used for performance assessments.”“If we’re building agents to help people complete everyday tasks using computers, our models need real examples of how people actually use them — things like mouse movements, clicking buttons, and navigating dropdown menus,” Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to The Verge. “To help, we’re launching an internal tool that will capture these kinds of inputs on certain applications to help us train our models. There are safeguards in place to protect sensitive content, and the data is not used for any other purpose.”RelatedMeta is reportedly planning to lay off thousands of workers in May.A rogue AI led to a serious security incident at MetaAn internal memo from Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth shared with employees on Monday announced plans to increase internal data collection, including with MCI, for Meta’s Agent Transformation Accelerator (ATA). Bosworth stated that, “The vision we are building towards is ​one where our agents primarily do the work and our role is to direct, review and help them improve.”According to Alex Heath’s Sources, Meta has experienced “intense internal backlash” over MCI, with one employee replying to the announcement memo saying, “This makes me super uncomfortable. How do we opt out?” Bosworth replied with, “There is no option to opt out of this on your work provided laptop.”Update, April 22nd: Added note about internal employee reactions to MCI.
items.author: Stevie Bonifield
items.titleZh: Meta即将追踪员工电脑活动以训练AI代理
items.descriptionZh:
Meta公司正利用员工在工作中的操作行为训练其人工智能模型。据路透社报道，Meta已在美国员工的办公电脑上部署名为“模型能力计划”（Model Capability Initiative, MCI）的工具，该工具在员工使用工作相关应用和网站时运行，记录鼠标移动、点击、键盘输入以及偶尔的屏幕截图。

这些数据将用于训练Meta的AI模型，使其能更自然地模拟人类与计算机的交互方式，最终实现自动化完成类似Meta员工日常执行的工作任务。路透社指出，MCI收集的数据“不会用于员工绩效评估”。

Meta发言人Tracy Clayton在给The Verge的声明中解释道：“如果我们希望构建能帮助人们通过计算机完成日常任务的智能体，我们的模型就需要真实的人类操作范例——例如鼠标移动、按钮点击和下拉菜单导航。为此，我们推出了这款内部工具，在特定应用中捕获此类输入以辅助模型训练。我们已设置防护措施保护敏感内容，且数据不会用于其他目的。”

Meta首席技术官Andrew Bosworth在周一发给员工的内部备忘录中宣布，将加强内部数据收集（包括通过MCI工具），以支持公司的“智能体转型加速器”（Agent Transformation Accelerator, ATA）计划。Bosworth写道：“我们构建的愿景是让智能体承担主要工作，而人类的角色转向指挥、审核并帮助它们改进。”

然而据记者Alex Heath的消息来源透露，MCI在内部引发了“强烈的反弹”。有员工在公告备忘录下回复称：“这让我感到极度不适。我们如何退出？”Bosworth对此回应：“公司提供的办公笔记本电脑无法退出该计划。”

此次数据收集计划与Meta近期推动AI代理开发的战略密切相关。公司此前曾经历由AI系统漏洞导致的安全事件，并据报道计划在五月进行大规模裁员。此次MCI工具的部署进一步凸显了Meta在提升AI能力过程中，对内部数据资源的深度整合与伦理边界之间的张力。

（更新于4月22日：补充员工对MCI的内部反应信息）

items.title: Alienware Area-51 gaming desktop now available with AMD’s flagship Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition — top CPU config starts at $4,449.99 with RTX 5070 and 32GB DDR5
items.description: Alienware is the first to launch a system powered by AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition, combining the new flagship CPU with high-end components.

Subscribe to our newsletter With AMD releasing its flagship Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition CPU, Alienware has become the first PC brand to debut the chip on its Area-51 gaming desktop. According to the company, it is the most powerful Alienware desktop that they have ever built powered by an AMD processor. Naturally, it doesn't come cheap with pricing starting at $4,449.99 for a config with AMD's new chip, but one has to consider the $900 MSRP of the Ryzen 9 9950X3D2.Check out the Alienware Area-51 from Dell.comIn addition to the existing Ryzen 9000 series configurations, the Area-51 desktop lineup will offer the new Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 CPU, AMD’s most ambitious consumer desktop CPU. It features 16 cores, 32 threads, and a massive cache of 208MB, making it one of the best CPUs for gaming. The CPU will be paired with a standard ATX Alienware X870E motherboard available with up to 64GB of dual-channel DDR5 XMP memory at 6400 MT/s. For graphics, Alienware sticks with Nvidia’s RTX 5000 series, ranging from the RTX 5070 to the RTX 5090. Storage options include 1TB/2TB NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 5 SSD or a 4TB NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 4 SSD. Depending on the configuration, the Area-51 can be paired with either an 850W Gold or 1500W Platinum power supply, along with options for a 240mm or 360mm AIO liquid cooler.We recently tested the Area-51 with top-of-the-line Intel and AMD configurations and found it to be a noteworthy improvement over the company’s previous gaming desktop offerings. It features a rather large 80-liter chassis measuring 22.4 x 24.04 x 9.12 inches (569 x 610.5 x 231.6 mm), with a ‘Lunar Silver’ finish and a glass side panel to showcase the internals. It uses standard PC hardware, meaning you’re not fully restricted to OEM parts if you plan to upgrade in the future. However, you may need to purchase a $35 adapter kit for the included AlienwareFX daughterboard.There’s plenty of I/O available, including two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C ports, two USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A ports, and dedicated microphone-in and line-out ports at the front. At the back, there are two USB 4.0 Type-C ports, two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C ports, one USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A port, five USB 2.0 Type-A ports, and additional line-in, line-out, and SPDIF audio ports. The system also includes a Realtek RTL8125BG 2.5G Ethernet controller, along with a MediaTek Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 wireless networking chip.As expected from any Alienware product, there’s a large RGB light ring around the air intake, along with additional lighting inside the case. Apart from the included CPU liquid cooler, the case comes with dual 180mm fans at the front and two 140mm fans at the bottom.The base configuration, priced at $4,299.99, includes a 1TB SSD, 32GB of RAM, an Nvidia RTX 5070, an 850W PSU, and a 240mm liquid cooler. That said, Alienware’s system configurator recommends opting for the 1500W PSU and 360mm liquid cooler combo, which bumps the price up to $4,449.99. At the top end, a fully maxed-out configuration can go all the way up to $7,049.99. items.titleZh: Alienware Area-51游戏台式机现已搭载AMD旗舰级Ryzen 9 9950X3D2双核版上市——顶配机型起售价4,449.99美元，含RTX 5070显卡与32GB DDR5内存。
items.descriptionZh:
随着AMD正式发布其旗舰处理器Ryzen 9 9950X3D2，知名高端PC品牌Alienware（外星人）率先在其Area-51系列游戏台式机中搭载了这款芯片，成为首个推出该配置的品牌机。Alienware宣称，这是其有史以来基于AMD平台打造的性能最强大的台式机。当然，其价格也相当高昂，基础配置起售价为4,449.99美元，这需要考虑到Ryzen 9 9950X3D2处理器本身高达900美元的官方建议零售价。

在硬件配置方面，Area-51台式机产品线在现有Ryzen 9000系列配置基础上，新增了搭载AMD这款最具雄心的消费级桌面CPU——Ryzen 9 9950X3D2的选项。该处理器拥有16个核心、32个线程以及高达208MB的高速缓存，这使其成为当前最适合游戏的顶级CPU之一。与之搭配的是标准的ATX规格Alienware X870E主板，最高支持64GB双通道DDR5 XMP内存，速度可达6400 MT/s。显卡方面，Alienware坚持采用NVIDIA的RTX 5000系列，可选范围从RTX 5070到旗舰RTX 5090。存储选项包括1TB或2TB的NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 5 SSD，或者4TB的PCIe Gen 4 SSD。根据配置不同，整机可搭配850W金牌或1500W白金认证电源，以及240mm或360mm的一体式水冷散热器。

近期对Area-51顶配版（搭载英特尔和AMD顶级配置）的测试表明，该机型相比Alienware以往的台式机产品有显著提升。其机箱体积达80升，尺寸为22.4 x 24.04 x 9.12英寸，采用“月球银”配色并配有侧透玻璃面板以展示内部硬件。值得注意的优点是，它使用了标准PC硬件，这意味着用户未来升级时不会完全受限于OEM专用部件。不过，若想升级，可能需要额外购买一个35美元的适配器套件，用于连接机箱自带的AlienwareFX功能子板。

在I/O接口方面，该机前面板提供了两个USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A端口以及独立的麦克风输入和音频输出接口。后部接口更为丰富，包括两个USB 4.0 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C端口、一个USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A端口、五个USB 2.0 Type-A端口，以及额外的线路输入、线路输出和SPDIF音频接口。网络方面，系统集成了Realtek RTL8125BG 2.5G有线网卡，以及支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4的联发科无线网络芯片。

作为Alienware产品的标志，该机在进气口周围设计了一个大型RGB灯环，机箱内部也配备了额外的灯光效果。散热系统除了一体式CPU水冷散热器外，机箱前部标配两个180mm风扇，底部配备两个140mm风扇。

具体到配置与价格，基础配置（售价4,299.99美元）包括1TB SSD、32GB内存、NVIDIA RTX 5070显卡、850W电源和240mm水冷。然而，Alienware的系统配置器推荐用户选择1500W电源与360mm水冷散热器的组合，这将价格提升至4,449.99美元。而完全顶配的选项，价格最高可达7,049.99美元。

此次Alienware Area-51首发搭载Ryzen 9 9950X3D2，不仅巩固了其在高端游戏台式机市场的地位，也展现了AMD在顶级消费级处理器领域持续的技术推进。这款机型主要面向追求极致游戏性能、内容创作和专业应用的用户群体，其模块化设计也为硬核玩家提供了未来的升级灵活性。

items.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
items.description: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated for more than a decade, co‑engineering a full‑stack AI platform that spans every technology layer — from performance‑optimized libraries and frameworks to enterprise‑grade cloud services.  This foundation enables developers, startups and enterprises to push agentic and physical AI out of the lab and into production — from agents that […]
items.content: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated for more than a decade, co‑engineering a full‑stack AI platform that spans every technology layer — from performance‑optimized libraries and frameworks to enterprise‑grade cloud services. This foundation enables developers, startups and enterprises to push agentic and physical AI out of the lab and into production — from agents that manage complex workflows to robots and digital twins on the factory floor. At Google Cloud Next this week in Las Vegas, the partnership reaches a new milestone, with advancements to expand Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer for AI factories that will power the next frontier of agentic and physical AI. These include the new NVIDIA Vera Rubin-powered A5X bare-metal instances; a preview of Google Gemini on Google Distributed Cloud running on NVIDIA Blackwell and NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs; confidential VMs with NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs; and agentic AI on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the NVIDIA NeMo framework. Next-Generation Infrastructure: From NVIDIA Blackwell to Vera Rubin At Google Cloud Next, Google announced A5X powered by NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems, which — through extreme codesign across chips, systems and software — deliver up to 10x lower inference cost per token and 10x higher token throughput per megawatt than the prior generation. A5X will use NVIDIA ConnectX-9 SuperNICs, combined with next-generation Google Virgo networking, scaling to up to 80,000 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs within a single site cluster and up to 960,000 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs in a multisite cluster, enabling customers to run their largest AI workloads on NVIDIA‑optimized infrastructure. “At Google Cloud, we believe the next decade of AI will be shaped by customers’ ability to run their most demanding workloads on a truly integrated, AI‑optimized infrastructure stack,” said Mark Lohmeyer, vice president and general manager of AI and computing infrastructure at Google Cloud. “By combining Google Cloud’s scalable infrastructure and managed AI services with NVIDIA’s industry‑leading platforms, systems and software, we’re giving customers flexibility to train, tune and serve everything from frontier and open models to agentic and physical AI workloads — while optimizing for performance, cost and sustainability.” Google Cloud’s broad NVIDIA Blackwell portfolio ranges from A4 VMs with NVIDIA HGX B200 systems to rack-scale A4X VMs with NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 and A4X Max NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems, all the way to fractional G4 VMs with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs. Customers can right-size their acceleration capabilities, whether using multiple interconnected NVL72 racks that scale out to tens of thousands of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, a single rack that can scale up to 72 Blackwell GPUs with fifth-generation NVIDIA NVLink and NVLink 5 Switch, or just one-eighth of a GPU. This comprehensive platform helps teams optimize every workload, from mixture-of-experts reasoning, multimodal inference and data processing to complex simulations for the next frontier of physical AI and robotics. Leading frontier AI labs are already putting this infrastructure to work. Thinking Machines Lab is scaling its Tinker application programming interface (API) on A4X Max VMs with GB300 NVL72 systems to accelerate training, while OpenAI is running large‑scale inference on NVIDIA GB300 (A4X Max VMs) and GB200 NVL72 systems (A4X VMs) on Google Cloud for some of its most demanding inference workloads, including for ChatGPT. Secure AI Wherever It Needs to Run: Sovereign and Confidential Google Gemini models running on NVIDIA Blackwell and Blackwell Ultra GPUs are now in preview on Google Distributed Cloud, so customers can bring Google’s frontier models wherever their most sensitive data resides. NVIDIA Confidential Computing with the NVIDIA Blackwell platform enables Gemini models to run in a protected environment where prompts and fine‑tuning data stay encrypted and can’t be seen or altered by unauthorized parties, including the infrastructure operators. In the public cloud, the preview of Confidential G4 VMs with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs brings these protections to multi‑tenant environments — helping safeguard prompts, AI models and data so customers in regulated industries can access the power of AI without compromising on security or performance. This is the first confidential computing offering of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs in the cloud, giving Google Cloud customers a new foundation for secure, high‑performance AI. Open Models and APIs for Agentic AI The NVIDIA platform on Google Cloud is optimized to run every kind of model — from Google’s frontier Gemini and Gemma families to NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the broader open weight ecosystem — equipping developers to build agentic AI systems that reason, plan and act. NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super is available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, giving developers a direct path to discovering, customizing and deploying NVIDIA‑optimized reasoning and multimodal models for agentic workflows. Google Cloud and NVIDIA are also making it easier to train and customize open models at scale. Managed Training Clusters on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform introduced a new managed reinforcement learning (RL) API built with NVIDIA NeMo RL for accelerating RL training at scale while automating cluster sizing, failure recovery and job execution, so teams can focus on agent behavior and model quality instead of infrastructure management. Cybersecurity leader CrowdStrike uses NVIDIA NeMo open libraries such as NeMo Data Designer, NeMo Automodel and NeMo Megatron Bridge to generate synthetic data and fine-tuning Nemotron and other open large language models for domain-specific cybersecurity. Running on Managed Training Clusters on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, these capabilities accelerate threat detection, investigation and response. Building the Future of Industrial and Physical AI Building industrial and physical AI at scale demands powerful hardware and a combination of open models, libraries and frameworks to develop these complex end-to-end workflows. NVIDIA AI infrastructure, open models and physical AI libraries available on Google Cloud, is mainstreaming industrial and physical AI applications, enabling customers to simulate, optimize and automate real-world workflows. Solutions from leading industrial software providers, including Cadence and Siemens Digital Industries Software, are now available on Google Cloud, accelerated on NVIDIA AI infrastructure. These applications are powering the next-generation design, engineering and manufacturing of everything from chips to autonomous vehicles, robotics, aerospace platforms, heavy machinery and large-scale production systems. With NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and the open source NVIDIA Isaac Sim robotics simulation framework available on Google Cloud Marketplace, developers can build physically accurate digital twins and develop custom robotics simulations pipelines to train, simulate and validate robots before real-world deployment. NVIDIA NIM microservices for models like NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 can be deployed to Google Vertex AI and Google Kubernetes Engine. This enables robots and vision AI agents to see, reason and act in the physical world like humans, powering use cases such as automated data curation and annotation, advanced robot planning and reasoning, and intelligent video analytics agents for real-time insights and decision-making. Together, these technologies help developers seamlessly move from computer-aided design to living industrial digital twins and AI‑driven robots, accelerating processes from design sign‑off to factory optimization on the NVIDIA platform running on Google Cloud. Proven Impact: From Startups to Global Enterprises Global enterprises, AI labs and high‑growth startups are using NVIDIA and Google Cloud’s co-engineered platform to move from prototyping to production faster, including Snap, Schrödinger and Salesforce. Snap is cutting the cost of large‑scale A/B testing by shifting data pipelines to GPU‑accelerated Spark on Google Cloud. Schrödinger is shrinking weekslong drug discovery simulations into just hours with NVIDIA accelerated computing on Google Cloud. Startups are orchestrating the next wave of AI innovation — building new agents and AI‑native applications using NVIDIA accelerated computing on Google Cloud. As part of a broader ecosystem highlighted through NVIDIA Inception and Google for Startups, CodeRabbit and Factory are using NVIDIA Nemotron‑based models on Google Cloud to power code review and autonomous software development agents, while Aible, Mantis AI, Photoroom and Baseten are building enterprise data, video intelligence, generative imagery and managed inference solutions on the full‑stack NVIDIA platform on Google Cloud. More than 90,000 developers have become a part of the joint NVIDIA and Google Cloud developer community in just over a year, tapping this platform to build and scale new AI applications. In addition, NVIDIA has been honored at Next as Google Cloud Partner of the Year in two categories — AI Global Technology Partner and Infra Modernization Compute — in recognition of deep technical expertise and go-to-market alignment. Together, NVIDIA and Google Cloud are giving customers a cloud‑scale platform to turn experimental agents and simulations into production systems that review code, secure fleets, enable new AI applications and optimize factories in the real world. Learn more about the companies’ collaboration by attending NVIDIA sessions, demos and workshops at Google Cloud Next.
items.author: Ian Buck
items.titleZh: 英伟达与谷歌云携手合作，推进智能体AI与物理AI发展
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达（NVIDIA）与谷歌云（Google Cloud）长达十余年的合作已共同构建了一个覆盖全技术栈的AI平台，从性能优化的库和框架到企业级云服务。这一基础使开发者、初创公司和企业能够将智能体AI（agentic AI）与物理AI从实验室推向生产环境，涵盖从管理复杂工作流的智能体到工厂车间的机器人与数字孪生。在拉斯维加斯举行的谷歌云Next大会上，双方合作迈入新里程碑，通过一系列升级扩展谷歌云AI Hypercomputer，以支撑下一代智能体与物理AI的前沿应用。

**下一代基础设施：从Blackwell到Vera Rubin**
谷歌云宣布推出由英伟达Vera Rubin NVL72机架级系统驱动的A5X裸金属实例。通过芯片、系统与软件的深度协同设计，其每令牌推理成本比前代降低高达10倍，每兆瓦令牌吞吐量提升高达10倍。A5X采用英伟达ConnectX-9 SuperNIC，结合下一代谷歌Virgo网络，单站点集群可扩展至8万颗英伟达Rubin GPU，多站点集群可达96万颗，使客户能在英伟达优化基础设施上运行最大规模的AI工作负载。

谷歌云AI与计算基础设施副总裁Mark Lohmeyer表示，未来十年AI的发展将取决于客户能否在真正集成、AI优化的基础设施栈上运行最苛刻的工作负载。通过将谷歌云的可扩展基础设施、托管AI服务与英伟达领先的平台、系统及软件结合，客户能够灵活地训练、调优和部署从前沿模型、开源模型到智能体及物理AI工作负载的一切应用，同时优化性能、成本与可持续性。

谷歌云广泛的英伟达Blackwell产品组合涵盖从搭载HGX B200系统的A4虚拟机，到采用GB200 NVL72和GB300 NVL72系统的机架级A4X虚拟机，乃至配备RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell服务器版GPU的G4分时虚拟机。客户可根据需求灵活选择加速能力，无论是扩展至数万颗Blackwell GPU的多互联NVL72机架、通过第五代NVLink及NVLink 5交换机连接72颗Blackwell GPU的单机架，还是仅使用八分之一颗GPU。这一全面平台帮助团队优化各类工作负载，包括专家混合推理、多模态推理、数据处理以及面向物理AI与机器人前沿的复杂仿真。

目前，领先的前沿AI实验室已开始使用该基础设施。Thinking Machines Lab正基于搭载GB300 NVL72系统的A4X Max虚拟机扩展其Tinker API以加速训练；OpenAI则在谷歌云上使用GB300（A4X Max虚拟机）和GB200 NVL72系统（A4X虚拟机）运行大规模推理，包括ChatGPT的部分高需求推理任务。

**安全AI：随处运行，主权与机密性兼顾**
在谷歌分布式云（Google Distributed Cloud）上，基于英伟达Blackwell与Blackwell Ultra GPU运行的谷歌Gemini模型现已开放预览，使客户能在其最敏感数据所在处部署谷歌前沿模型。英伟达Blackwell平台的机密计算功能确保Gemini模型在受保护环境中运行，提示词与微调数据保持加密状态，未经授权方（包括基础设施运营商）无法查看或篡改。

在公有云中，搭载英伟达RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU的机密G4虚拟机预览版将这些保护机制引入多租户环境，帮助保护提示词、AI模型与数据，使受监管行业客户在保障安全与性能的前提下使用AI能力。这是英伟达Blackwell GPU在云端的首个机密计算产品，为谷歌云客户提供了安全高性能AI的新基础。

**开源模型与API赋能智能体AI**
谷歌云上的英伟达平台已优化支持各类模型，从谷歌前沿的Gemini和Gemma家族，到英伟达Nemotron开源模型及更广泛的开源权重生态系统，助力开发者构建能够推理、规划与行动的智能体AI系统。

Nemotron 3 Super现已登陆Gemini企业智能体平台，为开发者提供直接路径，以发现、定制和部署经英伟达优化的推理与多模态模型，用于智能体工作流。双方还简化了大规模训练与定制开源模型的流程：Gemini企业智能体平台上的托管训练集群推出了新的托管强化学习API，该API基于英伟达NeMo RL构建，可加速大规模强化学习训练，同时自动化集群规模调整、故障恢复与作业执行，让团队专注于智能体行为与模型质量，而非基础设施管理。

网络安全领军企业CrowdStrike使用NeMo Data Designer、NeMo Automodel和NeMo Megatron Bridge等开源库生成合成数据，并针对网络安全领域微调Nemotron及其他开源大语言模型。在搭载英伟达Blackwell GPU的Gemini企业智能体平台托管训练集群上运行，这些能力加速了威胁检测、调查与响应。

**构建工业与物理AI的未来**
大规模构建工业与物理AI需要强大硬件，以及结合开源模型、库与框架来开发复杂的端到端工作流。谷歌云提供的英伟达AI基础设施、开源模型及物理AI库，正推动工业与物理AI应用普及，使客户能够模拟、优化和自动化现实世界工作流。

包括Cadence和西门子数字工业软件在内的领先工业软件提供商，其解决方案现已登陆谷歌云，并基于英伟达AI基础设施加速。这些应用正推动从芯片到自动驾驶汽车、机器人、航空航天平台、重型机械及大规模生产系统的新一代设计、工程与制造。

随着英伟达Omniverse库及开源机器人仿真框架Isaac Sim在谷歌云市场上线，开发者可构建物理精确的数字孪生，并开发定制机器人仿真流水线，在真实部署前训练、仿真与验证机器人。英伟达NIM微服务（如用于Cosmos Reason 2模型）可部署至谷歌Vertex AI和谷歌Kubernetes引擎，使机器人与视觉AI智能体能像人类一样在物理世界中观察、推理与行动，支撑自动化数据整理与标注、高级机器人规划与推理、实时洞察与决策的智能视频分析等用例。

这些技术共同帮助开发者无缝实现从计算机辅助设计到活化的工业数字孪生及AI驱动机器人的跨越，在谷歌云运行的英伟达平台上加速从设计签核到工厂优化的全流程。

**实践成效：从初创公司到全球企业**
全球企业、AI实验室与高增长初创公司正利用英伟达与谷歌云共同设计的平台加速从原型到生产的进程，包括Snap、Schrödinger和Salesforce等。Snap通过将数据流水线迁移至谷歌云上GPU加速的Spark，降低大规模A/B测试成本；Schrödinger借助谷歌云上的英伟达加速计算，将长达数周的药物发现模拟缩短至几小时。

初创公司正引领下一波AI创新浪潮，利用谷歌云上的英伟达加速计算构建新型智能体与AI原生应用。作为英伟达初创加速计划与谷歌创业计划生态的一部分，CodeRabbit和Factory使用谷歌云上基于英伟达Nemotron的模型驱动代码审查与自主软件开发智能体；Aible、Mantis AI、Photoroom和Baseten则在谷歌云的全栈英伟达平台上构建企业数据、视频智能、生成图像及托管推理解决方案。

短短一年内，已有超过9万名开发者加入英伟达与谷歌云的联合开发者社区，利用该平台构建和扩展新AI应用。此外，英伟达在Next大会上荣获谷歌云年度合作伙伴两项大奖——AI全球技术合作伙伴与基础设施现代化计算，以表彰其深厚技术专精与市场战略协同。

英伟达与谷歌云正共同为客户提供一个云级平台，将实验性智能体与仿真转化为生产系统，实现代码审查、车队安防、新型AI应用赋能以及现实世界工厂优化。如需了解更多合作细节，可参与谷歌云Next大会上的英伟达专题会议、演示与研讨会。

items.titleZh: 仅需479元，即可拥有AMD锐龙5 9600X处理器、B850 Max主板与16GB DDR5-6000内存——低成本游戏主机的理想选择
items.descriptionZh:
在构建游戏电脑时，玩家往往会被高端旗舰部件的华丽参数所吸引，但并非所有人都有无限的预算。当前，像英伟达RTX 5090这样的显卡起售价高达3599美元，AMD最新的Ryzen 9 9950X3D2处理器也需要899美元，这使得整机配置很容易超过6000美元。对于希望以合理画质进行游戏且预算较为有限的用户来说，寻找高性价比的组件组合至关重要。

Newegg目前推出了一款极具吸引力的硬件捆绑套装，原价619.97美元，现仅售479.98美元，直接节省了139.99美元。该套装包含AMD Ryzen 5 9600X处理器、华硕B850 Max Gaming WiFi主板、16GB的Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000内存，并免费赠送一款价值89.99美元的酷冷至尊Elite Liquid 360一体式CPU水冷散热器。这为打造一台性能强劲且成本可控的游戏PC提供了一个出色的基础。

**核心技术组件解析：**

1.  **AMD Ryzen 5 9600X处理器**：作为首批基于Zen 5架构、采用AM5平台的中端处理器，它拥有6核12线程，热设计功耗（TDP）仅为65W，能效比出色。虽然其绝对游戏性能无法与Ryzen 9 9800X3D等顶级型号相比，但凭借极高的性价比，它在性能排行榜上仍位居前列，是预算型高性能构建的理想选择。

2.  **Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000内存**：容量为16GB，时序为38-38-78，CAS延迟为CL38，工作电压1.25伏。这个容量对于当前大多数游戏来说已经足够，除非同时运行大量消耗内存的应用程序或开启极多的浏览器标签页。未来如需升级，内存也是PC中最容易更换的部件之一。

3.  **华硕B850 Max Gaming WiFi主板**：该主板采用8+2+1相供电设计，保证了系统稳定运行。集成了Wi-Fi 6E无线网络和蓝牙5.3，有线网络为2.5Gb以太网。它提供两个M.2 SSD插槽，其中主插槽支持最新的PCIe Gen 5协议固态硬盘，为未来存储升级预留了空间。

4.  **酷冷至尊Elite Liquid 360 AIO散热器**：这是一项关键赠品，因为Ryzen 5 9600X处理器不附带原装散热器。这款360毫米规格的一体式水冷散热器足以确保处理器在高负载下保持低温运行。

**性能与价值分析：**
该套装的核心优势在于其卓越的“价格-性能”比。以不到480美元的价格，用户获得了构建一台现代游戏PC所需的核心平台（CPU、主板、内存和高效散热）。用户只需额外添加显卡、固态硬盘和机箱即可完成整机搭建。与动辄数千美元的旗舰配置相比，这套组合能以远低于后者的成本，提供令人满意的1080p乃至1440p分辨率游戏体验。

**应用场景与影响：**
此捆绑套餐主要面向预算有限的游戏玩家、学生群体以及寻求高性价比升级方案的DIY爱好者。它降低了组建一台性能达标游戏PC的入门门槛，使更多用户无需在价格和体验之间做出极端妥协。对于零售商和AMD而言，此类促销有助于快速推广基于AM5平台的新一代硬件，加速市场渗透。

总之，Newegg的这款硬件捆绑套装通过精选的中端高性能组件组合，在控制成本的同时提供了扎实的基础性能，是构建低成本游戏PC的绝佳起点。对于精打细算的玩家来说，这是一个不容错过的促销机会。

items.title: Valve VRAM hack may improve gaming on 4GB GPUs — testing showed mixed results in select titles, with FPS almost tripling in certain games
items.description: Valve’s VRAM hack can boost performance on 4GB GPUs, with testing showing FPS gains of up to 3x in some titles, though results vary widely depending on the game and settings.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter YouTube tester NJ Tech has just shown that 4GB GPU users are not entirely left out of Valve's recent VRAM hack. Earlier this month, we covered a literal game-changing VRAM hack by Valve’s Natalie Vock for Linux gamers that lets you give priority to current gaming tasks. The hack fixes long-standing issues where gaming tasks are evicted from VRAM to make room for low-priority background tasks when VRAM is running low. The announcement focused on 8GB GPUs — understandably so, as most modern games are graphically demanding, requiring at least that much VRAM for high-visual-fidelity gaming.However, this left 4GB GPU users wondering where they stood. At the time, it seemed like the options were: upgrade (for the love of God!), stick to older games (or lower graphics), or keep dealing with the visual glitches. Fortunately, YouTube gaming tech channel NJ tech demonstrated, through extensive testing with some recent gaming titles, that the options are not exhaustive — the VRAM hack does offer some improvement in 4GB GPU gaming, at least in terms of FPS in certain games at low graphics settings.The test setup was a 4GB Radeon RX 6500 XT running CatchOS, paired with 16GB of DDR4 RAM and a Ryzen 5 5600X with a stock cooler. The channel tested various recent titles in low- to medium-graphic settings, obtaining mixed results.Article continues below You may like Valve engineer shocks Linux community with game-changing VRAM hack for 8GB GPUs Valve says it misreported VRAM capacity on some graphics cards in Steam Hardware Surveys Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis Alan Wake II saw the most improvement, with average frame rates nearly tripling from 14 FPS to 41 FPS, while 1% lows significantly increased from 12 FPS to 28 FPS. Two other titles, Resident Evil: Requiem and Silent Hill, showed more modest improvements. Conversely, a bunch of other titles showed little to no improvement.Swipe to scroll horizontallyFPS Performance Comparison: Valve VRAM Patch Enabled vs Disabled (4GB GPU)Game TitleSettingsAvg. FPS: Patch Disabled | Patch Enabled1% Low FPS: Patch Disabled | Patch EnabledAlan Wake II1080p Low, FSR Quality14 | 4112 | 28Resident Evil: Requiem1080p Lowest, Max Scaling67 | 6836 | 56Silent Hill f1080p Low, TAA47 | 5034 | 35Hogwarts Legacy1080p Medium, TAA High60 | 6145 | 47Death Stranding 21080p Low, PICO Native34 | 3428 | 28Cyberpunk 20771080p Low, High Texture, No Upscale49 | 4940 | 40The results showed that the Valve VRAM hack may offer some benefits to 4GB GPUs in certain scenarios. Of course, we will need more testing to conclusively assess the benefits across a wide range of titles. However, it is safe to say that 4GB-GPU users are not entirely left out.It's important to note that the hack does not reduce a game's VRAM usage; it just optimizes it, ensuring the game gets priority access to the space. If you run a title that requires at least 6 GB of VRAM on a 4 GB GPU, the patch ensures that the game doesn't have to compete with background system processes for the available 4 GB of memory. However, the remaining 2 GB will still spill into the system RAM. items.author: Etiido Uko
items.titleZh: Valve显存优化方案或提升4GB显卡游戏性能——实测效果因游戏而异，部分游戏帧率提升近三倍
items.descriptionZh:
近日，YouTube科技频道NJ Tech通过实测证实，Valve公司此前针对Linux系统推出的显存（VRAM）优化技术，并非仅对8GB显存显卡有效，部分4GB显存显卡用户也能从中获益。

**背景与问题**
本月初，Valve工程师Natalie Vock公布了一项被视作“改变游戏规则”的显存管理优化技术（常被称为“VRAM hack”）。该技术主要解决Linux系统中长期存在的一个问题：当显存不足时，高优先级的游戏任务数据会被迫移出显存，为低优先级的后台任务腾出空间，从而导致游戏卡顿、帧率下降或出现视觉瑕疵。此前的宣传重点集中在8GB显存显卡上，这合情合理，因为多数现代游戏图形负载高，高画质下至少需要8GB显存。但这让大量仍在使用4GB显存显卡的用户感到困惑，似乎他们只剩下升级硬件、玩老游戏或降低画质、忍受画面问题这几个选择。

**技术原理与创新**
这项技术的核心创新点并非直接降低游戏本身的显存占用，而是优化了Linux系统的显存分配策略。它通过调整优先级，确保当前正在运行的游戏任务能够优先占用并保留在显存中，避免被非关键的系统后台进程“挤占”空间。简而言之，它是在有限的显存容量内，更智能地分配资源，确保游戏数据获得最高访问权限。

**性能测试与数据分析**
为了验证该技术对4GB显卡的实际效果，NJ Tech频道搭建了一套测试平台：使用4GB显存的AMD Radeon RX 6500 XT显卡，运行CatchOS系统，搭配16GB DDR4内存和Ryzen 5 5600X处理器。测试在多款近期发布的游戏中，以低至中画质设置进行，结果喜忧参半。

最显著的提升出现在《心灵杀手2》（Alan Wake II）中。在1080p低画质、FSR质量模式下，开启优化后，平均帧率从14 FPS飙升至41 FPS，提升了近两倍；1%最低帧率也从12 FPS大幅改善至28 FPS，游戏流畅度得到质的飞跃。
《生化危机：安魂曲》（Resident Evil: Requiem）和《寂静岭f》（Silent Hill f）也显示出一定程度的改善，尤其是前者的1%最低帧率从36 FPS提升至56 FPS，稳定性增强。
然而，在《霍格沃茨之遗》、《死亡搁浅2》和《赛博朋克2077》等游戏中，帧率提升微乎其微，甚至没有变化。

**综合影响与应用场景**
测试结果表明，Valve的这项显存优化技术确实能在特定场景下为4GB显存显卡带来性能提升，尤其是对于那些本身接近但未完全超出4GB显存需求的游戏。它有效地缓解了因后台进程争抢资源导致的卡顿问题。

但必须明确其局限性：该技术无法“无中生有”。如果一款游戏在特定设置下需要至少6GB显存，那么在4GB显卡上，优化技术只能确保游戏进程独占这4GB显存，不被后台干扰。超出显卡物理显存容量的部分数据，仍然会溢出到系统内存（RAM）中，而系统内存的访问速度远低于显存，因此性能瓶颈依然存在。对于显存需求远超4GB的3A大作，提升将非常有限。

**结论**
总而言之，这项技术为4GB显存显卡用户提供了一个有价值的软件优化选项，让他们在运行部分较新游戏时，无需立即升级硬件也能获得更好的体验，特别是在帧率稳定性方面。但它并非万能药，不能替代硬件升级以满足日益增长的显存需求。更广泛的游戏测试仍有待进行，以全面评估其效益范围。对于4GB显卡用户而言，他们并未被完全排除在这次技术进步带来的好处之外。

items.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
items.description:
Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained - sometimes to the point of condoning violence.
But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.
More than 60 percent of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology's development should slow down. Still, "when you just ask folks, 'w …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI companies and executives is unrestrained — sometimes to the point of condoning violence.But look at the issues that most campaigns are focused on, and AI is far less prevalent, experts say.More than 60 percent of both Republicans and Democrats polled by Ipsos earlier this year agree that the government should regulate AI for economic stability and public safety, and that the technology’s development should slow down. Still, “when you just ask folks, ‘what’s on your mind?’ AI and data centers aren’t rising to the top of the list — at least not yet,” says lead pollster for Ipsos Public Affairs Alec Tyson.For now, broad topics like the economy and immigration remain priorities for many voters. “There’s a certain amount of oxygen for the top issues that Americans have on their mind, and we are living in a very active moment,” Tyson says. “The amount of available space or potential for another issue to break through, it has to be a pretty acute or powerful concern. And we’re just not seeing that at the national level with AI yet.”There’s also a lack of clear partisan lines. Data Center Watch, a group that tracks data center projects and their opposition, found 55 percent of politicians who publicly opposed large projects were Republicans and 45 percent were Democrats. There’s also bipartisan concern over AI chatbot companions’ impact on kids. While Republican politicians have led the push to override state AI laws, there’s still disagreement within both partiesEven so, with months left until the election, debates — and outright fights — over AI are heating up. Tech executives warn that their companies will upend people’s lives — Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has warned AI could eliminate half of entry-level white collar jobs, and Palantir CEO Alex Karp has said that Democratic voters could see a hit to their economic power while “working-class, often male voters” benefit. Activists have pushed back. Most efforts are peaceful, including protests and messages to lawmakers. But some opposition has turned violent. Three suspects allegedly attacked Sam Altman’s home in two separate attacks over a matter of days, and some responses on social media have suggested the attacks were justified. Similar to the gleeful response from many in the public after the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO, the violence has highlighted a simmering frustration among Americans.Meanwhile, well-funded interest groups are already spending millions on lobbying. “A lot of political science has well-documented that everyday Americans in some ways follow the rhetoric or position of the leaders they align with,” Tyson says. Groups like Brad Carson’s Americans for Responsible Innovation are focused on educating policymakers about AI to prepare them for coming policy debates. Carson, a former Democratic congressman, opposes efforts to override state AI regulations and is also part of Public First Action, which is affiliated with super PACs (political action committees) dedicated to supporting candidates that will back public safeguards against AI. They’re an answer to Leading the Future, a super PAC funded primarily by OpenAI president Greg Brockman and tech investors Marc Andreessen and Ben Horowitz. Leading the Future has raised $140 million, according to Axios, while Public First Action has $50 million cash on hand — $20 million of which is from Anthropic.“They’ve never seen an issue rise up those ranks faster than AI”Data centers have already become a flashpoint at the local level. Opposition to these projects has blocked or delayed $64 billion worth of development across the country, according to Data Center Watch. At the federal level, lawmakers like Sen. Bernie Sanders (I-VT) and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY) support a pause on data center development.Candidates running on key AI platforms have already seen money from groups like LTF and PFA poured into their races. That’s what happened to New York State lawmaker Alex Bores, who is currently running for reelection and cosponsored a bill originally meant to add safety and transparency requirements for large AI model developers. Despite LTF’s larger war chest, Carson believes public opinion is on his side, and says now is the time to push back against efforts to block state regulations.Concerns over job loss could also rise to the top of voters’ AI concerns as soon as this summer, according to Brendan Steinhauser, CEO of The Alliance for Secure AI, a nonprofit that aims to “defend humanity” in the AI age. “Based on what the technology is doing, and based on what leaders in the industry are saying about the technology, I think that gives me a signal that it could happen really fast,” he says. Jobs impact is also a key concern for many in Gen Z, says Tyson.The Alliance runs an online tracker of layoffs attributed to AI. So far, it’s tallied over 110,000 job losses in the US. Many have been at large tech companies — 30,000 came from layoffs at Oracle alone. But Steinhauser believes the threat may soon become more broadly tangible, as job loss is expected to hit everything from the legal profession to general administrative jobs. “That’s when I think it’s going to really be a much more salient issue across the country,” he says.“Most politicians are just now waking up to how powerful public sentiment is”Carson says that he consistently hears from pollsters that “they’ve never seen an issue rise up those ranks faster than AI is.” While many voters may not spontaneously mention it, “if you introduce the idea of AI and then raise things like price concerns or job concerns, they’re very salient.” But it still may be hard to vote based on that. “The candidates themselves aren’t necessarily clearly differentiated on how they want to approach AI because it’s a nascent and emerging issue,” he says.If voters aren’t (yet) closely tuned into AI issues, why are industry leaders spending millions on campaigns? Experts say it’s because there’s still more to be gained. “That public story is a little bit different from who actually has power,” says Purdue University associate political science professor Daniel Schiff. Headlines about Anthropic’s decision to hold its ground against the Pentagon and mass domestic surveillance, for example, might not reach many American voters, he said, but could help with “positioning themselves with respect to the government.”Carson says AI is “a great issue to run on” because “most politicians are just now waking up to how powerful public sentiment is about guardrails on AI. But you’re going to see more and more people embrace it because an entrepreneurial politician sees the opening here.” Sure, the billionaires behind Leading the Future “will try to destroy you, but there’s a limit to that, right? They can’t destroy us all.”
items.author: Lauren Feiner
items.descriptionZh:
尽管美国公众对人工智能普遍存在担忧，并在地方层面对数据中心项目表现出强烈抵制，但在即将到来的大选中，AI尚未成为选民最优先关注的顶层议题。然而，围绕AI的立法博弈、游说竞争和公众焦虑正在急剧升温，预示着其可能在未来政治格局中扮演关键角色。

**背景与公众情绪的矛盾**
多项民意调查显示，超过60%的美国两党选民都认为政府应监管AI以维护经济稳定和公共安全，并支持放缓技术发展。社交媒体上对AI公司及高管的愤怒情绪甚至蔓延至对暴力行为的默许。地方社区对数据中心项目的抵制已导致全美价值640亿美元的项目受阻或延迟。然而，当被问及最关心的问题时，经济和移民等传统议题仍占据主导。益普索公共事务首席民调专家亚历克·泰森指出，AI要突破现有议题格局，需要更尖锐或强大的催化剂，目前在全国层面尚未达到这一程度。

**政治层面的复杂性与两党共识的缺失**
在AI议题上，清晰的党派界限尚未形成。追踪数据中心项目的组织“数据中心观察”发现，公开反对大型项目的政客中，共和党人与民主党人比例相当（55%对45%）。两党内部也存在分歧，例如在是否优先于州级AI法规的问题上。同时，两党选民都对AI聊天伴侣对儿童的影响表示担忧。这种跨党派、党内的复杂性使得AI尚未像其他议题那样被高度政治化。

**争议升级：从言论到行动与资金博弈**
随着大选临近，AI辩论日趋激烈。科技高管发出严峻警告，如Anthropic CEO达里奥·阿莫代预测AI可能淘汰半数初级白领工作，Palantir CEO亚历克斯·卡普则称民主党选民的经济实力可能受损。活动人士通过抗议等方式进行反击，但部分反对行动已演变为暴力，如对OpenAI CEO萨姆·奥尔特曼住所的袭击，社交媒体上对此类暴力的合理化回应，折射出公众中郁积的挫败感。

与此同时，资金雄厚的利益集团正投入巨资进行游说。由OpenAI总裁格雷格·布罗克曼等科技领袖资助的超级政治行动委员会“引领未来”已筹集1.4亿美元。与之对垒的是由前民主党国会议员布拉德·卡森参与、与Anthropic有关联的“公共第一行动”，其手握5000万美元资金。这些资金正涌入关键选区的竞选，例如纽约州议员亚历克斯·博雷斯的连任竞选，他共同发起了一项旨在为大型AI模型开发者增加安全与透明度要求的法案。

**未来焦点：就业冲击与政治机遇**
专家预测，对失业的担忧可能最早在今年夏天升至选民AI关切列表的顶端。“保障AI安全联盟”的CEO布伦丹·斯坦豪瑟指出，基于技术发展现状和行业领袖的言论，就业影响可能迅速显现。该联盟追踪的AI导致的美国裁员人数已超过11万，预计将从科技公司蔓延至法律、行政等更广泛的行业。泰森补充道，就业影响也是Z世代的关键关切。

尽管许多选民尚未主动聚焦AI，但民调专家向卡森反馈，“从未见过任何一个议题像AI这样快速攀升关注度”。一旦引入AI概念并关联价格或就业担忧，这些议题就会变得非常突出。然而，由于AI是一个新兴议题，候选人在此问题上的政策立场往往不够分明，这为“有创业精神的政客”提供了机会。卡森认为，AI是一个绝佳的竞选议题，因为大多数政客刚刚意识到公众对AI设置护栏的情绪有多强烈。

**结论：权力博弈与公众故事的差异**
专家指出，行业领袖之所以投入巨资，是因为其中仍有巨大利益可图。普渡大学政治学副教授丹尼尔·希夫表示，关于谁真正拥有权力的故事，与公众所见的故事有所不同。例如，Anthropic抵制 Pentagon 和大规模国内监控的新闻头条可能并未触达多数选民，但却有助于其“在与政府的关系中定位自己”。当前的博弈表明，AI的政治化进程正在加速，公众情绪、经济利益和政策制定之间的复杂互动，将决定AI在美国的未来发展轨迹。

history.title: Linux may be ending support for older network drivers due to influx of false AI-generated bug reports — maintenance has become too burdensome for old largely-unused systems
history.description: Linux kernel developers are reviewing a proposal to remove obsolete ISA and PCMCIA-era Ethernet drivers from the mainline kernel, citing rising maintenance burden from AI-driven bug reports and fuzzing. The change would cut around 27,000 lines of legacy code
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The Linux kernel community is currently debating a significant proposal that could see countless legacy network drivers purged from the mainline source code to combat an unsustainable surge in AI-driven bug reports. This development follows a patch series submitted by OG developer Andrew Lunn to the netdev mailing list earlier this week.Maintaining support for old hardware has always been a “thing” for Linux. However, thanks to AI-wielding “detectives,” the sheer number of reports is forcing a shift in the kernel’s long-standing philosophy. Developers must now choose between addressing countless low-quality or hallucinated reports on systems no one uses or focusing their limited time on modern, high-impact subsystems.Andrew Lunn argued that while support for aging ISA and PCMCIA-era hardware was once a low-maintenance endeavor, it has recently become a disproportionate burden due to newbies using AI and fuzzers to uncover theoretical defects in code that likely have no remaining active users.Article continues below You may like Linux devs start removing support for 37-year-old Intel 486 CPU Linux lays down the law on AI-generated code, says yes to Copilot, no to AI slop, and humans take the fall for mistakes Linux kernel 7.0 finally abandons the 28-year-old Intel 440BX chipset "These old drivers have not been much of a maintenance burden until recently,” writes Lunn. “Now there are more newbies are using AI and fuzzers to find issues, resulting in more work for Maintainers. Fixing these old drivers makes little sense if it is not clear they have users.” Linux patches to remove ancient network drivers from kernel source tree (Image credit: Linux Kernel Community)Lunn notes that many of the Ethernet devices date to the late 1900s and feature ISA or PCMCIA interfaces (although there are a few that debuted between 2001 and 2002). If accepted, Lunn's proposal would remove specific drivers from 3Com, AMD, SMSC, Cirrus Logic, Fujitsu, Xircom, and 8390-based hardware families, eliminating approximately 27,646 lines of code from the kernel source tree.More importantly, rather than nuking support all at once, Linux would handle the removal one patch at a time, meaning a user could restore any of these drivers if they still depend on them and are willing to step in as a maintainer. This approach would ensure that legacy systems are not permanently locked out, but no longer impose an ongoing maintenance burden by default. history.titleZh: Linux或因AI生成错误报告激增而停止支持旧网络驱动——老旧系统维护已不堪重负
history.descriptionZh:
Linux内核社区近期正围绕一项重大提案展开激烈讨论，该提案旨在从主线源代码中清除大量遗留的网络驱动程序，以应对由人工智能（AI）驱动的错误报告数量激增所带来的不可持续的压力。这一动向源于资深开发者Andrew Lunn本周早些时候向netdev邮件列表提交的一系列补丁。

长期以来，为老旧硬件提供支持一直是Linux内核的核心理念之一。然而，随着越来越多新手开发者利用AI工具和模糊测试（Fuzzers）进行代码检测，情况发生了根本性变化。这些“AI侦探”能够自动发现大量理论上的代码缺陷，导致针对那些可能已无活跃用户的古老硬件的错误报告数量呈爆炸式增长。这使得内核维护者面临艰难抉择：是将有限的开发时间耗费在修复这些低质量甚至可能是AI“幻觉”产生的、无关紧要的报告上，还是集中精力维护现代、高性能且影响广泛的核心子系统。

Andrew Lunn在提案中指出，尽管支持那些基于ISA和PCMCIA等上古接口的硬件曾是一项维护成本很低的工作，但近期它已变成了不成比例的负担。他写道：“这些旧驱动程序直到最近都不是什么维护负担。但现在有更多新手使用AI和模糊测试来发现问题，这给维护者带来了更多工作。如果无法明确这些驱动是否还有用户，修复它们就意义不大。”

具体而言，此次清理目标主要针对上世纪90年代末期及21世纪初（少数到2002年）的以太网设备驱动，涉及3Com、AMD、SMSC、Cirrus Logic、富士通、Xircom以及基于8390芯片的硬件系列。如果提案获得通过，预计将从内核源码树中删除约27,646行代码。

值得注意的是，Linux社区计划采取的并非“一刀切”的激进策略。移除工作将通过逐个提交补丁的方式稳步推进。这意味着，如果仍有用户依赖某个特定驱动，并且愿意站出来承担维护责任，他们可以恢复该驱动。这种渐进式方法既确保了那些仍在运行的遗留系统不会被永久拒之门外，又能在默认情况下将内核维护者从这些持续的低价值维护任务中解放出来，从而优化整体开发资源的分配。

这一事件标志着Linux开发文化在AI时代面临的新挑战：当自动化工具极大降低了发现潜在问题的门槛时，项目必须重新评估其“支持一切”的包容性哲学与维护效率之间的平衡。其影响深远，不仅关乎内核代码库的“瘦身”与现代化，也可能为其他大型开源项目如何应对AI生成的海量问题报告树立先例。最终，决策将引导开发资源流向对现代计算生态更具价值的创新与优化工作。

history.title: Now Meta will track what employees do on their computers to train its AI agents
history.description:
Meta employees' activity at work is now being used to train the company's AI agents. As reported by Reuters, Meta is installing a tool it calls Model Capability Initiative (MCI) on US-based employees' computers that runs in work-related apps and websites, recording mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and occasional screenshots. 
The data from this tool will be used to train the company's AI models to get better at interacting with computers the way humans do, including automating work tasks like those Meta's employees perform on the job. According to Reuters, the data from MCI won't be "used for performance assessments."
"If we're buildin …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: Meta employees’ activity at work is now being used to train the company’s AI agents. As reported by Reuters, Meta is installing a tool it calls Model Capability Initiative (MCI) on US-based employees’ computers that runs in work-related apps and websites, recording mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and occasional screenshots.The data from this tool will be used to train the company’s AI models to get better at interacting with computers the way humans do, including automating work tasks like those Meta’s employees perform on the job. According to Reuters, the data from MCI won’t be “used for performance assessments.”“If we’re building agents to help people complete everyday tasks using computers, our models need real examples of how people actually use them — things like mouse movements, clicking buttons, and navigating dropdown menus,” Meta spokesperson Tracy Clayton said in a statement to The Verge. “To help, we’re launching an internal tool that will capture these kinds of inputs on certain applications to help us train our models. There are safeguards in place to protect sensitive content, and the data is not used for any other purpose.”RelatedMeta is reportedly planning to lay off thousands of workers in May.A rogue AI led to a serious security incident at MetaAn internal memo from Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth shared with employees on Monday announced plans to increase internal data collection, including with MCI, for Meta’s Agent Transformation Accelerator (ATA). Bosworth stated that, “The vision we are building towards is ​one where our agents primarily do the work and our role is to direct, review and help them improve.”According to Alex Heath’s Sources, Meta has experienced “intense internal backlash” over MCI, with one employee replying to the announcement memo saying, “This makes me super uncomfortable. How do we opt out?” Bosworth replied with, “There is no option to opt out of this on your work provided laptop.”Update, April 22nd: Added note about internal employee reactions to MCI.
history.author: Stevie Bonifield
history.titleZh: Meta即将追踪员工电脑活动以训练AI代理
history.descriptionZh:
Meta公司正利用员工在工作中的操作行为训练其人工智能模型。据路透社报道，Meta已在美国员工的办公电脑上部署名为“模型能力计划”（Model Capability Initiative, MCI）的工具，该工具在员工使用工作相关应用和网站时运行，记录鼠标移动、点击、键盘输入以及偶尔的屏幕截图。

这些数据将用于训练Meta的AI模型，使其能更自然地模拟人类与计算机的交互方式，最终实现自动化完成类似Meta员工日常执行的工作任务。路透社指出，MCI收集的数据“不会用于员工绩效评估”。

Meta发言人Tracy Clayton在给The Verge的声明中解释道：“如果我们希望构建能帮助人们通过计算机完成日常任务的智能体，我们的模型就需要真实的人类操作范例——例如鼠标移动、按钮点击和下拉菜单导航。为此，我们推出了这款内部工具，在特定应用中捕获此类输入以辅助模型训练。我们已设置防护措施保护敏感内容，且数据不会用于其他目的。”

Meta首席技术官Andrew Bosworth在周一发给员工的内部备忘录中宣布，将加强内部数据收集（包括通过MCI工具），以支持公司的“智能体转型加速器”（Agent Transformation Accelerator, ATA）计划。Bosworth写道：“我们构建的愿景是让智能体承担主要工作，而人类的角色转向指挥、审核并帮助它们改进。”

然而据记者Alex Heath的消息来源透露，MCI在内部引发了“强烈的反弹”。有员工在公告备忘录下回复称：“这让我感到极度不适。我们如何退出？”Bosworth对此回应：“公司提供的办公笔记本电脑无法退出该计划。”

此次数据收集计划与Meta近期推动AI代理开发的战略密切相关。公司此前曾经历由AI系统漏洞导致的安全事件，并据报道计划在五月进行大规模裁员。此次MCI工具的部署进一步凸显了Meta在提升AI能力过程中，对内部数据资源的深度整合与伦理边界之间的张力。

（更新于4月22日：补充员工对MCI的内部反应信息）

history.title: Alienware Area-51 gaming desktop now available with AMD’s flagship Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition — top CPU config starts at $4,449.99 with RTX 5070 and 32GB DDR5
history.description: Alienware is the first to launch a system powered by AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition, combining the new flagship CPU with high-end components.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter With AMD releasing its flagship Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition CPU, Alienware has become the first PC brand to debut the chip on its Area-51 gaming desktop. According to the company, it is the most powerful Alienware desktop that they have ever built powered by an AMD processor. Naturally, it doesn't come cheap with pricing starting at $4,449.99 for a config with AMD's new chip, but one has to consider the $900 MSRP of the Ryzen 9 9950X3D2.Check out the Alienware Area-51 from Dell.comIn addition to the existing Ryzen 9000 series configurations, the Area-51 desktop lineup will offer the new Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 CPU, AMD’s most ambitious consumer desktop CPU. It features 16 cores, 32 threads, and a massive cache of 208MB, making it one of the best CPUs for gaming. The CPU will be paired with a standard ATX Alienware X870E motherboard available with up to 64GB of dual-channel DDR5 XMP memory at 6400 MT/s. For graphics, Alienware sticks with Nvidia’s RTX 5000 series, ranging from the RTX 5070 to the RTX 5090. Storage options include 1TB/2TB NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 5 SSD or a 4TB NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 4 SSD. Depending on the configuration, the Area-51 can be paired with either an 850W Gold or 1500W Platinum power supply, along with options for a 240mm or 360mm AIO liquid cooler.We recently tested the Area-51 with top-of-the-line Intel and AMD configurations and found it to be a noteworthy improvement over the company’s previous gaming desktop offerings. It features a rather large 80-liter chassis measuring 22.4 x 24.04 x 9.12 inches (569 x 610.5 x 231.6 mm), with a ‘Lunar Silver’ finish and a glass side panel to showcase the internals. It uses standard PC hardware, meaning you’re not fully restricted to OEM parts if you plan to upgrade in the future. However, you may need to purchase a $35 adapter kit for the included AlienwareFX daughterboard.There’s plenty of I/O available, including two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C ports, two USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A ports, and dedicated microphone-in and line-out ports at the front. At the back, there are two USB 4.0 Type-C ports, two USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C ports, one USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A port, five USB 2.0 Type-A ports, and additional line-in, line-out, and SPDIF audio ports. The system also includes a Realtek RTL8125BG 2.5G Ethernet controller, along with a MediaTek Wi-Fi 7 and Bluetooth 5.4 wireless networking chip.As expected from any Alienware product, there’s a large RGB light ring around the air intake, along with additional lighting inside the case. Apart from the included CPU liquid cooler, the case comes with dual 180mm fans at the front and two 140mm fans at the bottom.The base configuration, priced at $4,299.99, includes a 1TB SSD, 32GB of RAM, an Nvidia RTX 5070, an 850W PSU, and a 240mm liquid cooler. That said, Alienware’s system configurator recommends opting for the 1500W PSU and 360mm liquid cooler combo, which bumps the price up to $4,449.99. At the top end, a fully maxed-out configuration can go all the way up to $7,049.99. history.titleZh: Alienware Area-51游戏台式机现已搭载AMD旗舰级Ryzen 9 9950X3D2双核版上市——顶配机型起售价4,449.99美元，含RTX 5070显卡与32GB DDR5内存。
history.descriptionZh:
随着AMD正式发布其旗舰处理器Ryzen 9 9950X3D2，知名高端PC品牌Alienware（外星人）率先在其Area-51系列游戏台式机中搭载了这款芯片，成为首个推出该配置的品牌机。Alienware宣称，这是其有史以来基于AMD平台打造的性能最强大的台式机。当然，其价格也相当高昂，基础配置起售价为4,449.99美元，这需要考虑到Ryzen 9 9950X3D2处理器本身高达900美元的官方建议零售价。

在硬件配置方面，Area-51台式机产品线在现有Ryzen 9000系列配置基础上，新增了搭载AMD这款最具雄心的消费级桌面CPU——Ryzen 9 9950X3D2的选项。该处理器拥有16个核心、32个线程以及高达208MB的高速缓存，这使其成为当前最适合游戏的顶级CPU之一。与之搭配的是标准的ATX规格Alienware X870E主板，最高支持64GB双通道DDR5 XMP内存，速度可达6400 MT/s。显卡方面，Alienware坚持采用NVIDIA的RTX 5000系列，可选范围从RTX 5070到旗舰RTX 5090。存储选项包括1TB或2TB的NVMe M.2 PCIe Gen 5 SSD，或者4TB的PCIe Gen 4 SSD。根据配置不同，整机可搭配850W金牌或1500W白金认证电源，以及240mm或360mm的一体式水冷散热器。

近期对Area-51顶配版（搭载英特尔和AMD顶级配置）的测试表明，该机型相比Alienware以往的台式机产品有显著提升。其机箱体积达80升，尺寸为22.4 x 24.04 x 9.12英寸，采用“月球银”配色并配有侧透玻璃面板以展示内部硬件。值得注意的优点是，它使用了标准PC硬件，这意味着用户未来升级时不会完全受限于OEM专用部件。不过，若想升级，可能需要额外购买一个35美元的适配器套件，用于连接机箱自带的AlienwareFX功能子板。

在I/O接口方面，该机前面板提供了两个USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A端口以及独立的麦克风输入和音频输出接口。后部接口更为丰富，包括两个USB 4.0 Type-C端口、两个USB 3.2 Gen 2 Type-C端口、一个USB 3.2 Gen 1 Type-A端口、五个USB 2.0 Type-A端口，以及额外的线路输入、线路输出和SPDIF音频接口。网络方面，系统集成了Realtek RTL8125BG 2.5G有线网卡，以及支持Wi-Fi 7和蓝牙5.4的联发科无线网络芯片。

作为Alienware产品的标志，该机在进气口周围设计了一个大型RGB灯环，机箱内部也配备了额外的灯光效果。散热系统除了一体式CPU水冷散热器外，机箱前部标配两个180mm风扇，底部配备两个140mm风扇。

具体到配置与价格，基础配置（售价4,299.99美元）包括1TB SSD、32GB内存、NVIDIA RTX 5070显卡、850W电源和240mm水冷。然而，Alienware的系统配置器推荐用户选择1500W电源与360mm水冷散热器的组合，这将价格提升至4,449.99美元。而完全顶配的选项，价格最高可达7,049.99美元。

此次Alienware Area-51首发搭载Ryzen 9 9950X3D2，不仅巩固了其在高端游戏台式机市场的地位，也展现了AMD在顶级消费级处理器领域持续的技术推进。这款机型主要面向追求极致游戏性能、内容创作和专业应用的用户群体，其模块化设计也为硬核玩家提供了未来的升级灵活性。

history.title: NVIDIA and Google Cloud Collaborate to Advance Agentic and Physical AI
history.description: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated for more than a decade, co‑engineering a full‑stack AI platform that spans every technology layer — from performance‑optimized libraries and frameworks to enterprise‑grade cloud services.  This foundation enables developers, startups and enterprises to push agentic and physical AI out of the lab and into production — from agents that […]
history.content: NVIDIA and Google Cloud have collaborated for more than a decade, co‑engineering a full‑stack AI platform that spans every technology layer — from performance‑optimized libraries and frameworks to enterprise‑grade cloud services. This foundation enables developers, startups and enterprises to push agentic and physical AI out of the lab and into production — from agents that manage complex workflows to robots and digital twins on the factory floor. At Google Cloud Next this week in Las Vegas, the partnership reaches a new milestone, with advancements to expand Google Cloud AI Hypercomputer for AI factories that will power the next frontier of agentic and physical AI. These include the new NVIDIA Vera Rubin-powered A5X bare-metal instances; a preview of Google Gemini on Google Distributed Cloud running on NVIDIA Blackwell and NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra GPUs; confidential VMs with NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs; and agentic AI on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the NVIDIA NeMo framework. Next-Generation Infrastructure: From NVIDIA Blackwell to Vera Rubin At Google Cloud Next, Google announced A5X powered by NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems, which — through extreme codesign across chips, systems and software — deliver up to 10x lower inference cost per token and 10x higher token throughput per megawatt than the prior generation. A5X will use NVIDIA ConnectX-9 SuperNICs, combined with next-generation Google Virgo networking, scaling to up to 80,000 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs within a single site cluster and up to 960,000 NVIDIA Rubin GPUs in a multisite cluster, enabling customers to run their largest AI workloads on NVIDIA‑optimized infrastructure. “At Google Cloud, we believe the next decade of AI will be shaped by customers’ ability to run their most demanding workloads on a truly integrated, AI‑optimized infrastructure stack,” said Mark Lohmeyer, vice president and general manager of AI and computing infrastructure at Google Cloud. “By combining Google Cloud’s scalable infrastructure and managed AI services with NVIDIA’s industry‑leading platforms, systems and software, we’re giving customers flexibility to train, tune and serve everything from frontier and open models to agentic and physical AI workloads — while optimizing for performance, cost and sustainability.” Google Cloud’s broad NVIDIA Blackwell portfolio ranges from A4 VMs with NVIDIA HGX B200 systems to rack-scale A4X VMs with NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 and A4X Max NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 systems, all the way to fractional G4 VMs with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs. Customers can right-size their acceleration capabilities, whether using multiple interconnected NVL72 racks that scale out to tens of thousands of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, a single rack that can scale up to 72 Blackwell GPUs with fifth-generation NVIDIA NVLink and NVLink 5 Switch, or just one-eighth of a GPU. This comprehensive platform helps teams optimize every workload, from mixture-of-experts reasoning, multimodal inference and data processing to complex simulations for the next frontier of physical AI and robotics. Leading frontier AI labs are already putting this infrastructure to work. Thinking Machines Lab is scaling its Tinker application programming interface (API) on A4X Max VMs with GB300 NVL72 systems to accelerate training, while OpenAI is running large‑scale inference on NVIDIA GB300 (A4X Max VMs) and GB200 NVL72 systems (A4X VMs) on Google Cloud for some of its most demanding inference workloads, including for ChatGPT. Secure AI Wherever It Needs to Run: Sovereign and Confidential Google Gemini models running on NVIDIA Blackwell and Blackwell Ultra GPUs are now in preview on Google Distributed Cloud, so customers can bring Google’s frontier models wherever their most sensitive data resides. NVIDIA Confidential Computing with the NVIDIA Blackwell platform enables Gemini models to run in a protected environment where prompts and fine‑tuning data stay encrypted and can’t be seen or altered by unauthorized parties, including the infrastructure operators. In the public cloud, the preview of Confidential G4 VMs with NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPUs brings these protections to multi‑tenant environments — helping safeguard prompts, AI models and data so customers in regulated industries can access the power of AI without compromising on security or performance. This is the first confidential computing offering of NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs in the cloud, giving Google Cloud customers a new foundation for secure, high‑performance AI. Open Models and APIs for Agentic AI The NVIDIA platform on Google Cloud is optimized to run every kind of model — from Google’s frontier Gemini and Gemma families to NVIDIA Nemotron open models and the broader open weight ecosystem — equipping developers to build agentic AI systems that reason, plan and act. NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super is available on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, giving developers a direct path to discovering, customizing and deploying NVIDIA‑optimized reasoning and multimodal models for agentic workflows. Google Cloud and NVIDIA are also making it easier to train and customize open models at scale. Managed Training Clusters on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform introduced a new managed reinforcement learning (RL) API built with NVIDIA NeMo RL for accelerating RL training at scale while automating cluster sizing, failure recovery and job execution, so teams can focus on agent behavior and model quality instead of infrastructure management. Cybersecurity leader CrowdStrike uses NVIDIA NeMo open libraries such as NeMo Data Designer, NeMo Automodel and NeMo Megatron Bridge to generate synthetic data and fine-tuning Nemotron and other open large language models for domain-specific cybersecurity. Running on Managed Training Clusters on Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, these capabilities accelerate threat detection, investigation and response. Building the Future of Industrial and Physical AI Building industrial and physical AI at scale demands powerful hardware and a combination of open models, libraries and frameworks to develop these complex end-to-end workflows. NVIDIA AI infrastructure, open models and physical AI libraries available on Google Cloud, is mainstreaming industrial and physical AI applications, enabling customers to simulate, optimize and automate real-world workflows. Solutions from leading industrial software providers, including Cadence and Siemens Digital Industries Software, are now available on Google Cloud, accelerated on NVIDIA AI infrastructure. These applications are powering the next-generation design, engineering and manufacturing of everything from chips to autonomous vehicles, robotics, aerospace platforms, heavy machinery and large-scale production systems. With NVIDIA Omniverse libraries and the open source NVIDIA Isaac Sim robotics simulation framework available on Google Cloud Marketplace, developers can build physically accurate digital twins and develop custom robotics simulations pipelines to train, simulate and validate robots before real-world deployment. NVIDIA NIM microservices for models like NVIDIA Cosmos Reason 2 can be deployed to Google Vertex AI and Google Kubernetes Engine. This enables robots and vision AI agents to see, reason and act in the physical world like humans, powering use cases such as automated data curation and annotation, advanced robot planning and reasoning, and intelligent video analytics agents for real-time insights and decision-making. Together, these technologies help developers seamlessly move from computer-aided design to living industrial digital twins and AI‑driven robots, accelerating processes from design sign‑off to factory optimization on the NVIDIA platform running on Google Cloud. Proven Impact: From Startups to Global Enterprises Global enterprises, AI labs and high‑growth startups are using NVIDIA and Google Cloud’s co-engineered platform to move from prototyping to production faster, including Snap, Schrödinger and Salesforce. Snap is cutting the cost of large‑scale A/B testing by shifting data pipelines to GPU‑accelerated Spark on Google Cloud. Schrödinger is shrinking weekslong drug discovery simulations into just hours with NVIDIA accelerated computing on Google Cloud. Startups are orchestrating the next wave of AI innovation — building new agents and AI‑native applications using NVIDIA accelerated computing on Google Cloud. As part of a broader ecosystem highlighted through NVIDIA Inception and Google for Startups, CodeRabbit and Factory are using NVIDIA Nemotron‑based models on Google Cloud to power code review and autonomous software development agents, while Aible, Mantis AI, Photoroom and Baseten are building enterprise data, video intelligence, generative imagery and managed inference solutions on the full‑stack NVIDIA platform on Google Cloud. More than 90,000 developers have become a part of the joint NVIDIA and Google Cloud developer community in just over a year, tapping this platform to build and scale new AI applications. In addition, NVIDIA has been honored at Next as Google Cloud Partner of the Year in two categories — AI Global Technology Partner and Infra Modernization Compute — in recognition of deep technical expertise and go-to-market alignment. Together, NVIDIA and Google Cloud are giving customers a cloud‑scale platform to turn experimental agents and simulations into production systems that review code, secure fleets, enable new AI applications and optimize factories in the real world. Learn more about the companies’ collaboration by attending NVIDIA sessions, demos and workshops at Google Cloud Next.
history.author: Ian Buck
history.titleZh: 英伟达与谷歌云携手合作，推进智能体AI与物理AI发展
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达（NVIDIA）与谷歌云（Google Cloud）长达十余年的合作已共同构建了一个覆盖全技术栈的AI平台，从性能优化的库和框架到企业级云服务。这一基础使开发者、初创公司和企业能够将智能体AI（agentic AI）与物理AI从实验室推向生产环境，涵盖从管理复杂工作流的智能体到工厂车间的机器人与数字孪生。在拉斯维加斯举行的谷歌云Next大会上，双方合作迈入新里程碑，通过一系列升级扩展谷歌云AI Hypercomputer，以支撑下一代智能体与物理AI的前沿应用。

**下一代基础设施：从Blackwell到Vera Rubin**
谷歌云宣布推出由英伟达Vera Rubin NVL72机架级系统驱动的A5X裸金属实例。通过芯片、系统与软件的深度协同设计，其每令牌推理成本比前代降低高达10倍，每兆瓦令牌吞吐量提升高达10倍。A5X采用英伟达ConnectX-9 SuperNIC，结合下一代谷歌Virgo网络，单站点集群可扩展至8万颗英伟达Rubin GPU，多站点集群可达96万颗，使客户能在英伟达优化基础设施上运行最大规模的AI工作负载。

谷歌云AI与计算基础设施副总裁Mark Lohmeyer表示，未来十年AI的发展将取决于客户能否在真正集成、AI优化的基础设施栈上运行最苛刻的工作负载。通过将谷歌云的可扩展基础设施、托管AI服务与英伟达领先的平台、系统及软件结合，客户能够灵活地训练、调优和部署从前沿模型、开源模型到智能体及物理AI工作负载的一切应用，同时优化性能、成本与可持续性。

谷歌云广泛的英伟达Blackwell产品组合涵盖从搭载HGX B200系统的A4虚拟机，到采用GB200 NVL72和GB300 NVL72系统的机架级A4X虚拟机，乃至配备RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell服务器版GPU的G4分时虚拟机。客户可根据需求灵活选择加速能力，无论是扩展至数万颗Blackwell GPU的多互联NVL72机架、通过第五代NVLink及NVLink 5交换机连接72颗Blackwell GPU的单机架，还是仅使用八分之一颗GPU。这一全面平台帮助团队优化各类工作负载，包括专家混合推理、多模态推理、数据处理以及面向物理AI与机器人前沿的复杂仿真。

目前，领先的前沿AI实验室已开始使用该基础设施。Thinking Machines Lab正基于搭载GB300 NVL72系统的A4X Max虚拟机扩展其Tinker API以加速训练；OpenAI则在谷歌云上使用GB300（A4X Max虚拟机）和GB200 NVL72系统（A4X虚拟机）运行大规模推理，包括ChatGPT的部分高需求推理任务。

**安全AI：随处运行，主权与机密性兼顾**
在谷歌分布式云（Google Distributed Cloud）上，基于英伟达Blackwell与Blackwell Ultra GPU运行的谷歌Gemini模型现已开放预览，使客户能在其最敏感数据所在处部署谷歌前沿模型。英伟达Blackwell平台的机密计算功能确保Gemini模型在受保护环境中运行，提示词与微调数据保持加密状态，未经授权方（包括基础设施运营商）无法查看或篡改。

在公有云中，搭载英伟达RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell GPU的机密G4虚拟机预览版将这些保护机制引入多租户环境，帮助保护提示词、AI模型与数据，使受监管行业客户在保障安全与性能的前提下使用AI能力。这是英伟达Blackwell GPU在云端的首个机密计算产品，为谷歌云客户提供了安全高性能AI的新基础。

**开源模型与API赋能智能体AI**
谷歌云上的英伟达平台已优化支持各类模型，从谷歌前沿的Gemini和Gemma家族，到英伟达Nemotron开源模型及更广泛的开源权重生态系统，助力开发者构建能够推理、规划与行动的智能体AI系统。

Nemotron 3 Super现已登陆Gemini企业智能体平台，为开发者提供直接路径，以发现、定制和部署经英伟达优化的推理与多模态模型，用于智能体工作流。双方还简化了大规模训练与定制开源模型的流程：Gemini企业智能体平台上的托管训练集群推出了新的托管强化学习API，该API基于英伟达NeMo RL构建，可加速大规模强化学习训练，同时自动化集群规模调整、故障恢复与作业执行，让团队专注于智能体行为与模型质量，而非基础设施管理。

网络安全领军企业CrowdStrike使用NeMo Data Designer、NeMo Automodel和NeMo Megatron Bridge等开源库生成合成数据，并针对网络安全领域微调Nemotron及其他开源大语言模型。在搭载英伟达Blackwell GPU的Gemini企业智能体平台托管训练集群上运行，这些能力加速了威胁检测、调查与响应。

**构建工业与物理AI的未来**
大规模构建工业与物理AI需要强大硬件，以及结合开源模型、库与框架来开发复杂的端到端工作流。谷歌云提供的英伟达AI基础设施、开源模型及物理AI库，正推动工业与物理AI应用普及，使客户能够模拟、优化和自动化现实世界工作流。

包括Cadence和西门子数字工业软件在内的领先工业软件提供商，其解决方案现已登陆谷歌云，并基于英伟达AI基础设施加速。这些应用正推动从芯片到自动驾驶汽车、机器人、航空航天平台、重型机械及大规模生产系统的新一代设计、工程与制造。

随着英伟达Omniverse库及开源机器人仿真框架Isaac Sim在谷歌云市场上线，开发者可构建物理精确的数字孪生，并开发定制机器人仿真流水线，在真实部署前训练、仿真与验证机器人。英伟达NIM微服务（如用于Cosmos Reason 2模型）可部署至谷歌Vertex AI和谷歌Kubernetes引擎，使机器人与视觉AI智能体能像人类一样在物理世界中观察、推理与行动，支撑自动化数据整理与标注、高级机器人规划与推理、实时洞察与决策的智能视频分析等用例。

这些技术共同帮助开发者无缝实现从计算机辅助设计到活化的工业数字孪生及AI驱动机器人的跨越，在谷歌云运行的英伟达平台上加速从设计签核到工厂优化的全流程。

**实践成效：从初创公司到全球企业**
全球企业、AI实验室与高增长初创公司正利用英伟达与谷歌云共同设计的平台加速从原型到生产的进程，包括Snap、Schrödinger和Salesforce等。Snap通过将数据流水线迁移至谷歌云上GPU加速的Spark，降低大规模A/B测试成本；Schrödinger借助谷歌云上的英伟达加速计算，将长达数周的药物发现模拟缩短至几小时。

初创公司正引领下一波AI创新浪潮，利用谷歌云上的英伟达加速计算构建新型智能体与AI原生应用。作为英伟达初创加速计划与谷歌创业计划生态的一部分，CodeRabbit和Factory使用谷歌云上基于英伟达Nemotron的模型驱动代码审查与自主软件开发智能体；Aible、Mantis AI、Photoroom和Baseten则在谷歌云的全栈英伟达平台上构建企业数据、视频智能、生成图像及托管推理解决方案。

短短一年内，已有超过9万名开发者加入英伟达与谷歌云的联合开发者社区，利用该平台构建和扩展新AI应用。此外，英伟达在Next大会上荣获谷歌云年度合作伙伴两项大奖——AI全球技术合作伙伴与基础设施现代化计算，以表彰其深厚技术专精与市场战略协同。

英伟达与谷歌云正共同为客户提供一个云级平台，将实验性智能体与仿真转化为生产系统，实现代码审查、车队安防、新型AI应用赋能以及现实世界工厂优化。如需了解更多合作细节，可参与谷歌云Next大会上的英伟达专题会议、演示与研讨会。

With GPUs like the RTX 5090 selling at a minimum of $3599, and the latest AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 costing $899, it's easy to see builds surpassing $6K, or even more. If you still want to game with decent settings and have a much more frugal budget, however, then component combo bundles like this Newegg deal featuring an AMD Ryzen 5 9600X, Asus B850 Max Gaming WiFi, 16GB Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000 RAM, and a Cooler Master Elite Liquid 360 AIO CPU cooler for an amazing $479 are a great start. ● Grab this deal at Newegg With today's deal, you're saving $139.99 off the original $619.97 list price and picking up this bundle of components for just $479.98. Plus, you're also getting a free Cooler Master Elite Liquid 360 all-in-one CPU cooler worth $89.99. Save 23% ($139.99)Newegg | AMD Ryzen 5 9600X CPU, Asus B850 Max Motherboard, 16GB Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000 RAM, and a Cooler Master Elite Liquid 360 AIO CPU Cooler: was $619.97 now $479.98 at NeweggA stunning combo bundle that is the start of a potent, yet low-cost gaming PC build. View Deal The Ryzen 5 9600X was one of the first Zen 5 processors to release for AMD's new AM5 platform and is still a great processor now, thanks to an excellent price-to-performance ratio. With 6 cores and 12 threads, plus a lower TDP rating of just 65W, the 9600X is cool to run and power-efficient. As you can see from our benchmarking chart below, it might not compete with the 9800X3D for pure out-and-out gaming performance, but it's still up there in the chart, and at a fraction of the price. history.author: Stewart Bendle
history.titleZh: 仅需479元，即可拥有AMD锐龙5 9600X处理器、B850 Max主板与16GB DDR5-6000内存——低成本游戏主机的理想选择
history.descriptionZh:
在构建游戏电脑时，玩家往往会被高端旗舰部件的华丽参数所吸引，但并非所有人都有无限的预算。当前，像英伟达RTX 5090这样的显卡起售价高达3599美元，AMD最新的Ryzen 9 9950X3D2处理器也需要899美元，这使得整机配置很容易超过6000美元。对于希望以合理画质进行游戏且预算较为有限的用户来说，寻找高性价比的组件组合至关重要。

Newegg目前推出了一款极具吸引力的硬件捆绑套装，原价619.97美元，现仅售479.98美元，直接节省了139.99美元。该套装包含AMD Ryzen 5 9600X处理器、华硕B850 Max Gaming WiFi主板、16GB的Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000内存，并免费赠送一款价值89.99美元的酷冷至尊Elite Liquid 360一体式CPU水冷散热器。这为打造一台性能强劲且成本可控的游戏PC提供了一个出色的基础。

**核心技术组件解析：**

1.  **AMD Ryzen 5 9600X处理器**：作为首批基于Zen 5架构、采用AM5平台的中端处理器，它拥有6核12线程，热设计功耗（TDP）仅为65W，能效比出色。虽然其绝对游戏性能无法与Ryzen 9 9800X3D等顶级型号相比，但凭借极高的性价比，它在性能排行榜上仍位居前列，是预算型高性能构建的理想选择。

2.  **Team Group T-Force Delta DDR5-6000内存**：容量为16GB，时序为38-38-78，CAS延迟为CL38，工作电压1.25伏。这个容量对于当前大多数游戏来说已经足够，除非同时运行大量消耗内存的应用程序或开启极多的浏览器标签页。未来如需升级，内存也是PC中最容易更换的部件之一。

3.  **华硕B850 Max Gaming WiFi主板**：该主板采用8+2+1相供电设计，保证了系统稳定运行。集成了Wi-Fi 6E无线网络和蓝牙5.3，有线网络为2.5Gb以太网。它提供两个M.2 SSD插槽，其中主插槽支持最新的PCIe Gen 5协议固态硬盘，为未来存储升级预留了空间。

4.  **酷冷至尊Elite Liquid 360 AIO散热器**：这是一项关键赠品，因为Ryzen 5 9600X处理器不附带原装散热器。这款360毫米规格的一体式水冷散热器足以确保处理器在高负载下保持低温运行。

**性能与价值分析：**
该套装的核心优势在于其卓越的“价格-性能”比。以不到480美元的价格，用户获得了构建一台现代游戏PC所需的核心平台（CPU、主板、内存和高效散热）。用户只需额外添加显卡、固态硬盘和机箱即可完成整机搭建。与动辄数千美元的旗舰配置相比，这套组合能以远低于后者的成本，提供令人满意的1080p乃至1440p分辨率游戏体验。

**应用场景与影响：**
此捆绑套餐主要面向预算有限的游戏玩家、学生群体以及寻求高性价比升级方案的DIY爱好者。它降低了组建一台性能达标游戏PC的入门门槛，使更多用户无需在价格和体验之间做出极端妥协。对于零售商和AMD而言，此类促销有助于快速推广基于AM5平台的新一代硬件，加速市场渗透。

总之，Newegg的这款硬件捆绑套装通过精选的中端高性能组件组合，在控制成本的同时提供了扎实的基础性能，是构建低成本游戏PC的绝佳起点。对于精打细算的玩家来说，这是一个不容错过的促销机会。

Subscribe to our newsletter YouTube tester NJ Tech has just shown that 4GB GPU users are not entirely left out of Valve's recent VRAM hack. Earlier this month, we covered a literal game-changing VRAM hack by Valve’s Natalie Vock for Linux gamers that lets you give priority to current gaming tasks. The hack fixes long-standing issues where gaming tasks are evicted from VRAM to make room for low-priority background tasks when VRAM is running low. The announcement focused on 8GB GPUs — understandably so, as most modern games are graphically demanding, requiring at least that much VRAM for high-visual-fidelity gaming.However, this left 4GB GPU users wondering where they stood. At the time, it seemed like the options were: upgrade (for the love of God!), stick to older games (or lower graphics), or keep dealing with the visual glitches. Fortunately, YouTube gaming tech channel NJ tech demonstrated, through extensive testing with some recent gaming titles, that the options are not exhaustive — the VRAM hack does offer some improvement in 4GB GPU gaming, at least in terms of FPS in certain games at low graphics settings.The test setup was a 4GB Radeon RX 6500 XT running CatchOS, paired with 16GB of DDR4 RAM and a Ryzen 5 5600X with a stock cooler. The channel tested various recent titles in low- to medium-graphic settings, obtaining mixed results.Article continues below You may like Valve engineer shocks Linux community with game-changing VRAM hack for 8GB GPUs Valve says it misreported VRAM capacity on some graphics cards in Steam Hardware Surveys Nvidia RTX 5070 mobile GPU looks set to get more VRAM despite global memory crisis Alan Wake II saw the most improvement, with average frame rates nearly tripling from 14 FPS to 41 FPS, while 1% lows significantly increased from 12 FPS to 28 FPS. Two other titles, Resident Evil: Requiem and Silent Hill, showed more modest improvements. Conversely, a bunch of other titles showed little to no improvement.Swipe to scroll horizontallyFPS Performance Comparison: Valve VRAM Patch Enabled vs Disabled (4GB GPU)Game TitleSettingsAvg. FPS: Patch Disabled | Patch Enabled1% Low FPS: Patch Disabled | Patch EnabledAlan Wake II1080p Low, FSR Quality14 | 4112 | 28Resident Evil: Requiem1080p Lowest, Max Scaling67 | 6836 | 56Silent Hill f1080p Low, TAA47 | 5034 | 35Hogwarts Legacy1080p Medium, TAA High60 | 6145 | 47Death Stranding 21080p Low, PICO Native34 | 3428 | 28Cyberpunk 20771080p Low, High Texture, No Upscale49 | 4940 | 40The results showed that the Valve VRAM hack may offer some benefits to 4GB GPUs in certain scenarios. Of course, we will need more testing to conclusively assess the benefits across a wide range of titles. However, it is safe to say that 4GB-GPU users are not entirely left out.It's important to note that the hack does not reduce a game's VRAM usage; it just optimizes it, ensuring the game gets priority access to the space. If you run a title that requires at least 6 GB of VRAM on a 4 GB GPU, the patch ensures that the game doesn't have to compete with background system processes for the available 4 GB of memory. However, the remaining 2 GB will still spill into the system RAM. history.titleZh: Valve显存优化方案或提升4GB显卡游戏性能——实测效果因游戏而异，部分游戏帧率提升近三倍
history.descriptionZh:
近日，YouTube科技频道NJ Tech通过实测证实，Valve公司此前针对Linux系统推出的显存（VRAM）优化技术，并非仅对8GB显存显卡有效，部分4GB显存显卡用户也能从中获益。

**背景与问题**
本月初，Valve工程师Natalie Vock公布了一项被视作“改变游戏规则”的显存管理优化技术（常被称为“VRAM hack”）。该技术主要解决Linux系统中长期存在的一个问题：当显存不足时，高优先级的游戏任务数据会被迫移出显存，为低优先级的后台任务腾出空间，从而导致游戏卡顿、帧率下降或出现视觉瑕疵。此前的宣传重点集中在8GB显存显卡上，这合情合理，因为多数现代游戏图形负载高，高画质下至少需要8GB显存。但这让大量仍在使用4GB显存显卡的用户感到困惑，似乎他们只剩下升级硬件、玩老游戏或降低画质、忍受画面问题这几个选择。

**技术原理与创新**
这项技术的核心创新点并非直接降低游戏本身的显存占用，而是优化了Linux系统的显存分配策略。它通过调整优先级，确保当前正在运行的游戏任务能够优先占用并保留在显存中，避免被非关键的系统后台进程“挤占”空间。简而言之，它是在有限的显存容量内，更智能地分配资源，确保游戏数据获得最高访问权限。

**性能测试与数据分析**
为了验证该技术对4GB显卡的实际效果，NJ Tech频道搭建了一套测试平台：使用4GB显存的AMD Radeon RX 6500 XT显卡，运行CatchOS系统，搭配16GB DDR4内存和Ryzen 5 5600X处理器。测试在多款近期发布的游戏中，以低至中画质设置进行，结果喜忧参半。

最显著的提升出现在《心灵杀手2》（Alan Wake II）中。在1080p低画质、FSR质量模式下，开启优化后，平均帧率从14 FPS飙升至41 FPS，提升了近两倍；1%最低帧率也从12 FPS大幅改善至28 FPS，游戏流畅度得到质的飞跃。
《生化危机：安魂曲》（Resident Evil: Requiem）和《寂静岭f》（Silent Hill f）也显示出一定程度的改善，尤其是前者的1%最低帧率从36 FPS提升至56 FPS，稳定性增强。
然而，在《霍格沃茨之遗》、《死亡搁浅2》和《赛博朋克2077》等游戏中，帧率提升微乎其微，甚至没有变化。

**综合影响与应用场景**
测试结果表明，Valve的这项显存优化技术确实能在特定场景下为4GB显存显卡带来性能提升，尤其是对于那些本身接近但未完全超出4GB显存需求的游戏。它有效地缓解了因后台进程争抢资源导致的卡顿问题。

但必须明确其局限性：该技术无法“无中生有”。如果一款游戏在特定设置下需要至少6GB显存，那么在4GB显卡上，优化技术只能确保游戏进程独占这4GB显存，不被后台干扰。超出显卡物理显存容量的部分数据，仍然会溢出到系统内存（RAM）中，而系统内存的访问速度远低于显存，因此性能瓶颈依然存在。对于显存需求远超4GB的3A大作，提升将非常有限。

**结论**
总而言之，这项技术为4GB显存显卡用户提供了一个有价值的软件优化选项，让他们在运行部分较新游戏时，无需立即升级硬件也能获得更好的体验，特别是在帧率稳定性方面。但它并非万能药，不能替代硬件升级以满足日益增长的显存需求。更广泛的游戏测试仍有待进行，以全面评估其效益范围。对于4GB显卡用户而言，他们并未被完全排除在这次技术进步带来的好处之外。

history.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
history.description:
